title: EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED — Budget envelope consumed (design-time business event)
description:
DESIGN-TIME DOCUMENTATION ONLY — NO AUTONOMOUS BUS MESSAGE.

This schema describes the abstract business fact 'a budget envelope of a beneficiary has been (partially or fully) consumed' at the meta-model level (ADR-BCM-URBA-0007). Per ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 Rule 2, only the resource event (RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED) generates an autonomous bus message; this business event appears on the wire ONLY as the prefix of the routing key (Rule 4). It is therefore NEVER published as a standalone message on RabbitMQ; no producer should ever serialise a payload against this schema for transport, and no consumer should ever expect to receive one.

Why this schema still exists: it documents the business intent carried by the operational resource event, traces back to BCM business-event-reliever.yaml, supports human governance (DDD ubiquitous language), and feeds documentation tooling (EventCatalog views, Miro process boards) — without polluting the operational rail.

For the runtime, transported, validated payload see RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED.schema.json — the runtime contract.

Governing ADRs: ADR-BCM-FUNC-0008 (BSP.004 L2 breakdown), ADR-BCM-URBA-0007 (event meta-model), ADR-BCM-URBA-0009 (capability event responsibility), ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 (Dual-Rail Event Infrastructure — NORMATIVE).

Identity resolution: as for the paired resource event, consumer-side identity resolution from case_id toward OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id is performed via CAP.REF.001.BEN. This producer NEVER carries the canonical internal_id.

allocation_id.description: Identifier of the consumed envelope (one (case_id, period, category) triple). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.allocation_id.

case_id.description: CORRELATION KEY (design-time view). Reference to the beneficiary's participation case (Reliever case ID) owned by CAP.BSP.002.ENR. Identity resolution toward OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id is performed by consumers via CAP.REF.001.BEN — NEVER carried by the producer.

category.description: Spending category covered by the envelope (e.g. ALIMENTATION, TRANSPORT). Canonically held by CAP.REF.001.TIE.

cap_amount.description: Maximum amount authorised on the envelope for the active period.

consumed_amount.description: Total consumed amount of the envelope after the consumption that triggered this business fact. The available remaining balance is conceptually `cap_amount - consumed_amount`.

period_start.description: Inclusive start date of the envelope's active allocation period (ISO-8601 calendar date).

period_end.description: Inclusive end date of the envelope's active allocation period (ISO-8601 calendar date).

status.description: Status of the envelope at the time of the business fact (OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.status). Documents the lifecycle position; not transported on the wire.
